info.title: Unsiloed Parser API — v1
info.description: The original document parsing API. Accepts multipart file uploads and URL-based processing. These endpoints have no version prefix in their URLs and are stable indefinitely.

get.summary: List all API keys for the authenticated user (with masked keys)

post.summary: Create a new API key for the authenticated user

post.description:
Queues a document for processing and returns a TaskResponse containing:
- Task ID for status polling
- Initial configuration
- File metadata
- Processing status
- Creation timestamp
- Presigned URLs for file access

The returned task will typically be in a `Starting` or `Processing` state.
Use the `GET /task/{task_id}` endpoint to poll for completion.

requestBody.description: JSON request to create a task

get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a task by its ID, including:
- Processing status
- Task configuration
- Output data (if processing is complete)
- File metadata (name, page count)
- Timestamps (created, started, finished)
- Presigned URLs for accessing files

This endpoint can be used to:
1. Poll the task status during processing
2. Retrieve the final output once processing is complete
3. Access task metadata and configuration

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Id of the task to retrieve

parameters.name: base64_urls
parameters.description: Whether to return base64 encoded URLs. If false, the URLs will be returned as presigned URLs.

parameters.name: include_chunks
parameters.description: Whether to include chunks in the output response

parameters.name: output_file
parameters.description: Whether to return a presigned URL to the output file instead of the full output. When true, the response will include output_file_url pointing to the S3-stored result.

200.description: Detailed information describing the task, its status and processed outputs

500.description: Internal server error related to getting the task

delete.description:
Delete a task by its ID.

Requirements:
- Task must have status `Succeeded` or `Failed`

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Id of the task to delete

500.description: Internal server error related to deleting the task

get.description:
Cancel a task that hasn't started processing yet:
- For new tasks: Status will be updated to `Cancelled`
- For updating tasks: Task will revert to the previous state

Requirements:
- Task must have status `Starting`

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Id of the task to cancel

500.description: Internal server error related to cancelling the task

patch.description:
Updates an existing task's configuration and reprocesses the document.
The original configuration will be used for all values that are not provided in the update.

Requirements:
- Task must have status `Succeeded` or `Failed`
- New configuration must be different from the current one

The returned task will typically be in a `Starting` or `Processing` state.
Use the `GET /task/{task_id}` endpoint to poll for completion.

requestBody.description: JSON request to update an task

200.description: Detailed information describing the task, its status and processed outputs

500.description: Internal server error related to updating the task

get.description:
Retrieves a list of tasks

Example usage:
`GET /api/v1/tasks?page=1&limit=10&include_chunks=false`

parameters.name: base64_urls
parameters.description: Whether to return base64 encoded URLs. If false, the URLs will be returned as presigned URLs.

parameters.name: include_chunks
parameters.description: Whether to include chunks in the output response

500.description: Internal server error related to getting the task

get.description: Confirmation that the service can respond to requests

200.description: Confirmation that the service can respond to requests and the version of the service

post.description:
Create a document processing task using Vision-API compatible interface.
This endpoint accepts multipart form data with Vision-API parameter names
and maps them internally to Core Parser format.

Supports both file upload and URL input.

get.description:
Get the status and results of a processing task.
Returns task information in Vision-API compatible format.

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: Job ID returned from create parse endpoint

parameters.name: base64_urls
parameters.description: Whether to return base64 encoded URLs

parameters.name: include_chunks
parameters.description: Whether to include chunks in output

parameters.name: output_file
parameters.description: Whether to return presigned URL to output file

key_preview.description: Masked key showing only the last 4 characters

oneOf.description: Controls which source is used to populate the `content` field

extended_context.description: Use the full page image as context for LLM generation

BoundingBox.description: Bounding box for an item. It is used for chunks, segments and OCR results.

height.description: The height of the bounding box.

left.description: The left coordinate of the bounding box.

top.description: The top coordinate of the bounding box.

width.description: The width of the bounding box.

chunk_id.description: The unique identifier for the chunk.

chunk_length.description: The total number of tokens in the chunk. Calculated by the `tokenizer`.

embed.description:
Suggested text to be embedded for the chunk. This text is generated by combining the embed content
from each segment according to the configured embed sources (HTML, Markdown, LLM, or Content).
Can be configured using `embed_sources` in the `SegmentProcessing` configuration.

segments.description:
Collection of document segments that form this chunk.
When `target_chunk_length` > 0, contains the maximum number of segments
that fit within that length (segments remain intact).
Otherwise, contains exactly one segment.

ChunkProcessing.description: Controls the setting for the chunking and post-processing of each chunk.

ignore_headers_and_footers.description: Whether to ignore headers and footers in the chunking process.

target_length.description: The target number of words in each chunk. If 0, each chunk will contain a single segment.

oneOf.description: The tokenizer to use for the chunking process.

detect_checkboxes.description: Whether to detect checkboxes using Swin Transformer Detection API

expires_in.description:
The number of seconds until task is deleted.
Expired tasks can **not** be updated, polled or accessed via web interface.

extract_colors.description:
Whether to extract and apply color information from PDF text layer to OCR results.
When enabled, text color (RGB/hex) from the PDF is transferred to matching OCR words.
Default: false

extract_links.description:
Whether to extract and apply hyperlink annotations from PDF to OCR results.
When enabled, link URLs from PDF annotations are attached to matching OCR words.
Default: false

high_resolution.description: Whether to use high-resolution images for cropping and post-processing.

input_file_url.description: The presigned URL of the input file.

merge_tables.description: Whether to merge adjacent tables using VLM

output_fields.description:
Configuration for controlling which output fields are included in responses.
Allows selective inclusion/exclusion of HTML, Markdown, OCR, images, etc.

page_numbers.description:
Explicit mapping of array indices to actual page numbers (1-based).
Used when parsing selective pages (e.g., [4, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12]).
Populated from the page_range parameter.

page_range.description: Optional page range to process (e.g., "1-5,8,10-12"). If None, all pages are processed.

segment_type_naming.description: Controls the naming convention for segment types in the output

target_chunk_length.description: The target number of words in each chunk. If 0, each chunk will contain a single segment.

xml_citation.description: Whether to extract citations using Grobid

ContentSource.description: Controls which source is used to populate the `content` field in the segment output

warning.description: Warning message about storing the key securely

detect_checkboxes.description: Detect checkboxes using Swin Transformer Detection API.

expires_in.description:
The number of seconds until task is deleted.
Expired tasks can **not** be updated, polled or accessed via web interface.

extract_colors.description:
Extract and apply color information from PDF text layer to OCR results.
When enabled, text color (RGB/hex) from the PDF is transferred to matching OCR words.

extract_links.description:
Extract and apply hyperlink annotations from PDF to OCR results.
When enabled, link URLs from PDF annotations are attached to matching OCR words.

file.description: The file to be uploaded. Can be a URL or a base64 encoded file.

file_name.description: The name of the file to be uploaded. If not set a name will be generated.

high_resolution.description: Whether to use high-resolution images for cropping and post-processing. (Latency penalty: ~7 seconds per page)

oneOf.description: Configuration for controlling which output fields are included in responses

page_range.description:
Page range to parse (supports multiple formats):
- Single page: "2" → page 2
- Range: "2-5" → pages 2, 3, 4, 5
- Comma-separated: "2,4,6" → pages 2, 4, 6
- JSON array: "[2,4,6]" → pages 2, 4, 6

oneOf.description: Segment analysis configuration (alias for segment_processing, Vision-API compatible)
Segment processing configuration (preferred name)

validate_segments.description: List of segment types to validate using VLM (e.g. ["Table", "Formula", "Picture"])

validate_table_segments.description: Legacy: enable VLM validation for table segments (use validate_segments instead)

xml_citation.description: Extract structured citation data using Grobid.

detect_checkboxes.description: Detect checkboxes using Swin Transformer Detection API.

expires_in.description:
The number of seconds until task is deleted.
Expired tasks can **not** be updated, polled or accessed via web interface.

high_resolution.description: Whether to use high-resolution images for cropping and post-processing. (Latency penalty: ~7 seconds per page)

oneOf.description: Segment analysis configuration (alias for segment_processing, Vision-API compatible)
Segment processing configuration (preferred name)

xml_citation.description: Extract structured citation data using Grobid.

CroppingStrategy.description:
- `All` crops all images and includes URLs in response
- `Auto` crops images if needed for LLM processing and includes URLs in response
- `None` crops images if needed for LLM processing but does NOT include URLs in response

ErrorHandlingStrategy.description:
Controls how errors are handled during processing:
- `Fail`: Stops processing and fails the task when any error occurs
- `Continue`: Attempts to continue processing despite non-critical errors (eg. LLM refusals etc.)

oneOf.description: No fallback will be used
Use the system default fallback model
Use a specific model as fallback

Model.description: Use a specific model as fallback

FallbackStrategy.description:
Specifies the fallback strategy for LLM processing

This can be:
1. None - No fallback will be used
2. Default - The system default fallback model will be used
3. Model - A specific model ID will be used as fallback (check the documentation for the models.)

oneOf.description: Controls which source is used to populate the `content` field (default: OCR)

extended_context.description: Use the full page image as context for LLM generation

use_table_ocr.description: Use Surya OCR for table processing to provide structured context to LLM (default: false)

LlmProcessing.description: Controls the LLM used for the task.

fallback_strategy.description: The fallback strategy to use for the LLMs in the task.

max_completion_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate.

model_id.description:
The ID of the model to use for the task. If not provided, the default model will be used.
Please check the documentation for the model you want to use.

temperature.description: The temperature to use for the LLM.

oneOf.description: Optional text color (RGB) provided by OCR engine

confidence.description: The confidence score of the recognized text.

link.description: Optional link URL associated with this OCR result (from PDF annotations)

text.description: The recognized text of the OCR result.

OcrEngine.description:
Controls which OCR engine to use for text recognition:
- `UnsiloedStorm`: Uses Google Cloud Vision API (enterprise-grade accuracy, 50+ languages)
- `UnsiloedHawk`: Uses the Unsiloed/Surya OCR engine (higher accuracy, better for complex layouts)
- `UnsiloedBeta`: Uses PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 via vLLM (0.9B VLM, irregular bboxes, rotated/warped text) [DEFAULT]

OcrStrategy.description:
Controls the Optical Character Recognition (OCR) strategy.
- `All`: Processes all pages with OCR. (Latency penalty: ~0.5 seconds per page)
- `Auto`: Selectively applies OCR only to pages with missing or low-quality text. When text layer is present the bounding boxes from the text layer are used.

OutputFieldsConfig.description:
Configuration for controlling which fields are included in the API response.
By default, all fields are included for backward compatibility.
Set specific fields to false to exclude them from the response.

bbox.description: Include bounding box information in segment responses

chart_data.description: Include extracted chart data for Picture segments identified as charts

confidence.description: Include confidence scores in segment responses

content.description: Include text content in segment responses

embed.description: Include embed text in chunk responses

html.description: Include HTML representation in segment responses

image.description: Include images (cropped segment images) in segment responses

llm.description: Include LLM-generated content in segment responses

markdown.description: Include Markdown representation in segment responses

ocr.description: Include OCR results in segment responses

OutputResponse.description: The processed results of a document analysis task

chunks.description: Collection of document chunks, where each chunk contains one or more segments

extracted_json.description: The extracted JSON from the document.

output_file_url.description: The presigned URL of the output JSON file stored in S3 (when output_file=true)

page_count.description: The number of pages in the file.

pdf_url.description: The presigned URL of the PDF file.

PictureCroppingStrategy.description:
Controls the cropping strategy for an item (e.g. segment, chunk, etc.)
- `All` crops all images and includes URLs in response
- `Auto` crops images if needed for LLM processing and includes URLs in response
- `None` crops images if needed for LLM processing but does NOT include URLs in response

oneOf.description: Controls which source is used to populate the `content` field (default: OCR)

extended_context.description: Use the full page image as context for LLM generation

chart_data.description:
Extracted chart data for Picture segments identified as charts.
Contains structured data including chart_type, data series, labels, and legend.

confidence.description: Confidence score of the layout analysis model

content.description:
Text content of the segment. By default, this contains OCR-extracted text.
The source can be configured via the `content_source` field in segment processing configuration
to use HTML, Markdown, or LLM representation instead.

image.description: Presigned URL to the image of the segment.

page_height.description: Height of the page containing the segment.

page_width.description: Width of the page containing the segment.

segment_type_display.description: Display name for the segment type (used for serialization based on naming convention)

SegmentType.description:
All the possible types for a segment.
Note: Different configurations will produce different types.
Please refer to the documentation for more information.

SegmentTypeNamingConvention.description:
Controls the naming convention for segment types in the output:
- `Unsiloed`: Uses Unsiloed naming convention (e.g., `PageHeader`, `PageFooter`, `SectionHeader`, `ListItem`, `Picture`)
- `Other`: Uses alternative naming convention (e.g., `Header`, `Footer`, `Section Header`, `List Item`, `Figure`)

SegmentationStrategy.description:
Controls the segmentation strategy:
- `LayoutAnalysis`: Analyzes pages for layout elements (e.g., `Table`, `Picture`, `Formula`, etc.) using bounding boxes. Provides fine-grained segmentation and better chunking. (Latency penalty: ~TBD seconds per page).
- `Page`: Treats each page as a single segment. Faster processing, but without layout element detection and only simple chunking.

created_at.description: The date and time when the task was created and queued.

expires_at.description: The date and time when the task will expire.

finished_at.description: The date and time when the task was finished.

message.description: A message describing the task's status or any errors that occurred.

started_at.description: The date and time when the task was started.

task_id.description: The unique identifier for the task.

task_url.description: The presigned URL of the task.

Tokenizer.description:
Common tokenizers used for text processing.

These values represent standard tokenization approaches and popular pre-trained
tokenizers from the Hugging Face ecosystem.

oneOf.description: Use one of the predefined tokenizer types

Enum.description: Use one of the predefined tokenizer types

oneOf.description:
Use any Hugging Face tokenizer by specifying its model ID
Examples: "Qwen/Qwen-tokenizer", "facebook/bart-large"

String.description:
Use any Hugging Face tokenizer by specifying its model ID
Examples: "Qwen/Qwen-tokenizer", "facebook/bart-large"

TokenizerType.description:
Specifies which tokenizer to use for the chunking process.

This type supports two ways of specifying a tokenizer:
1. Using a predefined tokenizer from the `Tokenizer` enum
2. Using any Hugging Face tokenizer by providing its model ID as a string
   (e.g. "facebook/bart-large", "Qwen/Qwen-tokenizer", etc.)

When using a string, any valid Hugging Face tokenizer ID can be specified,
which will be loaded using the Hugging Face tokenizers library.
